data.description:
## Findings

**Symptom**: compactor-518c stuck in `ready_for_implementation_review` for 2.5+ hours despite the agent
correctly running `kno next`. Take loop iter 3 (OpenCode kimi-k2.6, lease compactor-ff16, PID 99910) never
spawns iter 4. The OpenCode child stays alive emitting `file.watcher.updated` SSE events every ~30s.

**Live forensic evidence** (session term-1777542580150-h9pdxk, .foolery-logs/compactor/2026-04-30/):
- 09:51:51.912 OpenCode child spawned, prompt POST'd to /session/X/message
- 09:51:53.480 SSE stream flowing (assistant, message.part.delta...)
- 09:56:53.982 sendMessage POST fails (`http_message_request_failed` — undici headersTimeout 300s elapsed)
- 09:56:53.983 emitErrorResult fires `{type:"step_finish",part:{reason:"error"}}` → normalizer maps to
  `{type:"result",is_error:true}` → first turn_ended fires (state still `implementation`)
- 09:56:54.031 take_loop_follow_up_sent ("advance or rollback") — spurious
- 09:59:31 agent finishes original work, advances state to ready_for_implementation_review
- 10:00:43.259 follow-up POST finally completes → prompt_delivery_succeeded
- 10:00:43.261 turn_ended fires from session.idle (real turn-end, morning fix wiring works)
- 10:00:43.263 turn_ended fires from session.status type=idle (duplicate from morning fix)
- 10:00:43.304 last normalized_event_observed for the session
- After this: only stderr_observed events forever. NO child_close, NO terminate-process-group, NO
  invariant rollback. Take loop hangs. Knot stays in queue.

## Root Cause

**Bug A (the actual hang)**: src/lib/agent-session-runtime-events.ts:75 — processNormalizedEvent calls
\`doCancelInputClose(state)\` unconditionally on every normalized event. After session.idle fires
turn_ended → state.resultObserved=true → doScheduleInputClose schedules close at T+2000ms. Within that
2s window, more SSE events arrive (post-idle message.updated flush, file.watcher.updated, etc.) — each
flows through processNormalizedEvent → doCancelInputClose → timer killed before it fires. No more
turn_ended events to reschedule. Child stays alive forever.

Why only OpenCode: stdio (Claude) and jsonrpc (Codex) go silent after result/turn.completed. OpenCode
HTTP's /event SSE endpoint is a long-lived stream that NEVER goes silent.

Why the morning fix (49a17082) exposed this: pre-fix, step_finish fired turn_ended on every model step
so the loop sent constant follow-ups, each new prompt called resetForNewTurn → resultObserved=false →
close kept getting properly rescheduled. Morning fix correctly stops that thrashing — but now
resultObserved stays true after the one real session.idle and the cancellation race becomes terminal.

**Bug B (the spurious follow-up)**: src/lib/opencode-http-session.ts:265 — when sendMessage times out
(undici headersTimeout default 300s), doTurn calls emitErrorResult which synthesizes
\`{type:\"step_finish\",part:{reason:\"error\"}}\`. This fires turn_ended with is_error:true even though
the agent is still working — its progress is on the SSE stream which the morning fix correctly treats as
canonical. The synthetic error triggers a take-loop follow-up while the real turn is still running.

## Implementation Requirements

**Change 1 (Bug A)**: src/lib/agent-session-runtime-events.ts processNormalizedEvent — replace
unconditional doCancelInputClose with: \`if (!state.resultObserved) doCancelInputClose(state);\`. Add a
comment naming this knot and the failure mode. resetForNewTurn already sets resultObserved=false on
follow-up prompt send, so cancellation re-engages cleanly when a new turn begins.

**Change 2 (Bug B)**: src/lib/opencode-http-session.ts doTurn — when \`sendMessage\` returns null on the
HTTP POST path, call hooks.onFailed and cb.onError BUT DO NOT call emitErrorResult. SSE is the canonical
turn-end signal; the POST response is redundant once the SSE stream is up. Keep emitErrorResult for the
\"http_session_not_ready\" branch (we never sent the message, so no SSE will ever deliver session.idle).

**Hermetic tests** (src/lib/__tests__/, follow Hermetic Test Policy in CLAUDE.md):
1. processNormalizedEvent does NOT call doCancelInputClose when state.resultObserved=true
2. processNormalizedEvent DOES call doCancelInputClose when state.resultObserved=false
3. After resetForNewTurn, doCancelInputClose is callable again (resultObserved=false)
4. Bug A regression: simulate session.idle turn_ended followed by post-idle normalized event within 2s
   and assert closeInputTimer is still set (i.e., the close will fire)
5. doTurn on sendMessage-returns-null does NOT emit step_finish reason=error event
6. doTurn on \"http_session_not_ready\" DOES emit step_finish reason=error event (regression for the
   genuinely-broken case)

**Code style**: enforce CLAUDE.md limits — 500 lines/file, 100 lines/function, 100 cols/line. Run
\`bun run lint\`, \`bunx tsc --noEmit\`, \`bun run test\`, \`bun run build\` before shipping.

**Live verification** (manual, post-merge): kill stuck OpenCode PID 99910, observe a fresh OpenCode
take-loop iteration completes — child_close fires within ~5s of session.idle, take loop advances to next
iteration. Session log shows no \"http_message_request_failed\" → spurious follow-up cascade.

## Out of Scope

- Reducing the undici fetch timeout itself (separate concern; we fix the FALLOUT of the timeout, not the
  timeout itself)
- Reverting the morning fix 49a17082 (it is correct; the bugs being fixed are pre-existing, just exposed)
- Generic transport refactor — minimal changes only
